rationale: Both notes indicate a close conceptual overlap in advertising message revelation: the cited paper studies mystery/upfront ad identification over exposure duration and effects on ad/brand attitudes, while the citing paper tests a tease-then-reveal uncertainty format and its effects on brand attitude and willingness to try. The citing note explicitly says it “complements prior advertising work” on uncertainty-based delivery, so this looks plausibly foundational/comparative rather than an obvious incidental mention.
